title: typha angustifolia subsp. domingensis
intro: Typha angustifolia subsp. domingensis, also known as the narrow-leaved cattail, is a subspecies of the cattail plant that is native to the Caribbean and parts of Central and South America. The narrow-leaved cattail has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. The narrow-leaved cattail can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
